incidents.desc: Dialog work process pool reached 89% during month-end batch window. ZeroOps detected saturation pattern, rescheduled background jobs to reduce dialog pool pressure, and executed SM04 session cleanup. All 847 WPs released. Finance team unaffected.
incidents.notes: Month-end batch jobs scheduled in dialog class competed with 1,200 concurrent user sessions. Background WP class had 40% headroom. ZeroOps rescheduled 12 batch jobs via SM37. Dialog pool dropped from 89% to 41% within 4 minutes. No user impact recorded.

intel.rootCause: Batch jobs scheduled in wrong WP class competing with dialog sessions during peak period
intel.recommendation: Implement automated WP class scheduling rules — reschedule background jobs during period-end windows automatically

chain: 12 batch jobs allocated to dialog WP class
signals: WP utilisation alert from SAP Cloud ALM

runbook.action: Alert correlation — SAP Cloud ALM WP utilisation signal
Identify batch jobs in dialog WP class via SM37

incidents.desc: EDI gateway message queue stalled with 2,341 messages pending across ORDERS, DESADV and INVOIC message types. 9 of 12 trading partner connections dropped following overnight network maintenance. ZeroOps detected queue buildup, reconnected partner sessions and initiated ordered message replay.
incidents.notes: Network maintenance at 02:00 dropped persistent TCP connections to EDI trading partners. Reconnection logic in EDI gateway v3.2.1 had a known issue with bulk reconnection. ZeroOps executed sequential partner reconnection over 8 minutes, then replayed queued messages in order. All 2,341 messages delivered by 08:51.

intel.rootCause: EDI gateway bulk reconnection failure following planned network maintenance
intel.recommendation: Upgrade EDI gateway to v3.4+ which resolves bulk reconnection defect. Interim: add ZeroOps reconnection rule post-maintenance-window.

chain: Network maintenance at 02:00 — all TCP sessions dropped
EDI gateway bulk reconnection attempted at 02:15
v3.2.1 bulk reconnect defect caused all 12 connections to fail
Queue depth grew from 0 to 2,341 over 6 hours
ZeroOps detected at 08:22 via queue depth threshold

runbook.action: Queue depth detection and alert correlation
Replay queued messages in chronological order

incidents.desc: Jenkins build agent disk reached 91%. ZeroOps executed automated cleanup of build artefacts older than 14 days, freed 38GB, build queue clearing.
incidents.notes: Build artefact retention policy not enforced on agent 04 following config drift. ZeroOps cleanup executed at 07:30. Disk from 91% to 52%. 34 queued builds released.

intel.rootCause: Build artefact retention policy config drift on Jenkins agent 04
intel.recommendation: Enforce retention policy via Ansible playbook across all agents. Add ZeroOps daily disk check.

cause.primary: Config drift — retention policy not applied
chain: Retention policy applied to agents 01-03 in August
Agent 04 missed due to patching skip

incidents.desc: SSO authentication latency elevated to 4.2s average. RADIUS backend investigating. ZeroOps has identified RADIUS server response time degradation as root cause and is preparing remediation.
incidents.notes: RADIUS server response time increased from 120ms to 3.8s at 08:52. Correlates with certificate renewal on RADIUS-02. ZeroOps RCA Engine identified certificate-related TLS renegotiation as primary cause.

intel.rootCause: RADIUS server TLS renegotiation overhead following certificate renewal
intel.recommendation: Restart RADIUS service to clear TLS session cache. Review certificate renewal procedure to avoid session disruption.

cause.primary: TLS session cache invalidation post certificate renewal
chain: TLS session cache cleared on renewal
All new authentications requiring full TLS handshake

runbook.action: Confirm RADIUS TLS renegotiation via packet trace
Schedule RADIUS service restart — human approval required (auth impact)

incidents.desc: Database deadlock on STOCK_LEDGER table causing warehouse operations to stall. 14 warehouse operatives unable to process goods receipts. ZeroOps has identified the deadlock pattern and is awaiting HiTL approval to execute index rebuild.
incidents.notes: Concurrent update pattern on STOCK_LEDGER during high-volume goods receipt session. Missing composite index on (LOCATION_ID, ITEM_ID, TIMESTAMP) causing full table scan under load. ZeroOps recommends online index rebuild — 12-minute operation, warehouse operations suspended during rebuild.

intel.rootCause: Missing composite index on STOCK_LEDGER causing deadlocks under concurrent update load
intel.recommendation: Online index rebuild during low-traffic window. Long-term: review table partitioning strategy.

cause.primary: Missing composite index — full table scan under load
chain: Concurrent updates on STOCK_LEDGER without composite index
Full table scan causing lock contention

runbook.action: Human approval for index rebuild (operational impact)

incidents.desc: VPN gateway SSL certificate expires in 8 days. ZeroOps proactively detected and raised. Certificate renewal process initiated. Without action, 2,840 remote users will lose connectivity.
incidents.notes: Proactive detection via certificate monitoring — 8 days to expiry. Auto-renewal attempted but blocked by CA approval workflow. Human action required to approve renewal in certificate management portal.

intel.rootCause: Proactive — SSL certificate approaching expiry. No fault yet. Prevention action required.
intel.recommendation: Approve certificate renewal in CA portal within 48 hours. ZeroOps will install renewed certificate automatically once approved.

similar.resolution: Manual — same certificate, renewed 2 days before expiry

runbook.action: Certificate expiry detection — ZeroOps proactive monitor
CA portal approval — human action required

incidents.desc: API Gateway error rate elevated to 4.2%. Root cause: downstream WMS database deadlock causing timeout cascade. ZeroOps applied circuit breaker pattern and rerouted affected API calls.
incidents.notes: Cascading timeout from WMS DB deadlock. ZeroOps applied circuit breaker, returned graceful degradation response to calling applications, preventing full cascade failure. Error rate returned to 0.1% once WMS stabilised.

intel.rootCause: Cascading timeout from WMS database deadlock propagating to API Gateway
intel.recommendation: Implement circuit breaker pattern for WMS-dependent API routes as permanent configuration.

chain: API Gateway WMS calls timing out after 30s
Circuit breaker triggered — graceful degradation applied

runbook.action: Error rate detection and root cause correlation
Remove circuit breaker when upstream stable

incidents.desc: 847 messages in ESB dead letter queue — retry exhausted. ZeroOps has categorised messages by failure type and is preparing replay plan.
incidents.notes: Messages failed due to downstream EDI gateway outage (INC0001002). Now that EDI gateway is restored, messages are eligible for replay. ZeroOps has prepared ordered replay plan for human approval.

intel.rootCause: Downstream EDI gateway unavailability caused message processing failure — retry exhausted
intel.recommendation: Replay 847 messages in chronological order. All downstream systems now available.

cause.primary: Downstream dependency unavailable during message processing
chain: ESB message retry exhausted after 3 attempts

runbook.action: Categorise messages by type and failure reason
